questions.question: What is the main difference between supervised and unsupervised learning?
options: Supervised learning uses rules, while unsupervised learning uses data
Supervised learning uses labeled data, while unsupervised learning does not
Supervised learning is always more accurate than unsupervised learning
Unsupervised learning requires more labeled examples than supervised learning

questions.question: Which is a typical supervised learning task?
In unsupervised learning, what is the model trying to do?
options: Predict known answers from labeled examples
Discover structure and patterns in unlabeled data

questions.question: When is supervised learning often best?
options: When labels are unavailable and you want to explore the data
When labeling is expensive and slow
When you have reliable labels and want specific predictions
When you want to group similar items without predefined categories
